text: When developing a website, security should always be a top priority. One fundamental step is using HTTPS to encrypt data between the user's browser and the server. This prevents attackers from intercepting sensitive information like passwords. Speaking of passwords, implementing strong authentication is crucial. Users should create complex passwords, and developers can add an extra layer with two-factor authentication. Another important measure is input validation. Never trust data that comes from users; always check and sanitize it to prevent malicious code injections. Failing to do so can lead to common vulnerabilities such as SQL injection or cross-site scripting (XSS), which hackers exploit to steal information or take control of a site.
